purpose: Close the current candidate-relative SemanticSLAM map/pose top-rule path as non-promotable after the Docker task-proxy join, while preserving candidate-relative map/pose evidence only as a possible active-observation utility source.

closure_reason: task-label coverage is complete, so the blocker is not missing evaluation plumbing
the top map-pose candidate rule would commit all 50 request rows but yields 21 wrong-goal rows and 4 no-valid rows
the candidate-relative unique-top subset is still unsafe with 19 wrong-goal rows and 4 no-valid rows out of 42 rows
the candidate_relative_dominant_map_pose_support tag is not a valid goal-correctness authority because it has 23 correct, 19 wrong, and 4 no-valid rows
map/pose contrast currently measures candidate observability or geometric salience, not ObjectNav goal validity
revising the utility formula from this top-rule would tune around a demonstrated wrong-goal mechanism

surviving_evidence_role.reason: Candidate-relative map/pose evidence can expose uncertainty and rival ambiguity, but current rows show it cannot decide ObjectNav goal validity by itself.

revision_requirements.target_question: Can candidate-relative map/pose uncertainty choose active re-observation targets that reduce wrong-goal/no-valid risk without acting as a direct terminal selector?
revision_requirements.required_design_shift: Convert candidate-relative map/pose contrast into active-observation utility and risk-aware defer logic, not a direct top-candidate commit rule.
required_evidence_properties: action-time rows must output observe/defer priorities, not terminal commits
candidate-relative terms must be paired with a non-GT risk guard for no-valid and same-category rival ambiguity
map/pose-native terms must remain separated from semantic rank, detector score, source-top, local-context, oracle, and evaluation labels
the revision must report when map/pose contrast is high but goal-validity evidence is weak or contradictory
the revision must include simpler alternatives such as defer-all, current_unique_ready, top_map_pose_tuple, top_projection_visible_heading, and semantic/detector fallback audits
task labels may be joined only after action rows are frozen and only as evaluation-only diagnostics
promotion requires at least one map-side metric and one task-side proxy to improve together on held-out or fresh validation

next_research_task.first_question: Can the unsafe top-rule rows be converted into observe/defer priorities that target uncertainty instead of terminal goal selection?
next_research_task.reason_implementation_blocked: The active-observation utility definition must be frozen before any runtime materializer or formula revision.

interpretation_rule.fact: Candidate-relative map/pose evidence and task labels join with full source-row coverage, but top-rule and unique-top candidates still include wrong-goal and no-valid cases.
interpretation_rule.agent_inference: The current candidate-relative map/pose path is not a terminal utility path. Its defensible role is active observation or uncertainty diagnosis, not direct candidate commitment.
